card.intuition: An architectural style for designing networked applications based on resources, standard HTTP verbs, and stateless communication.
card.analogy: A library where every book has a unique ID (URL) and you use standard actions like 'Get', 'Add', 'Update', or 'Delete' to interact with them.
when_to_use: Building public APIs for mobile apps or web frontends
When cacheability and simplicity are preferred over complex queries
gotchas: REST is not a protocol (unlike SOAP or gRPC); it is a set of constraints and guidelines
Statelessness means every request must contain all the information needed to understand it; the server session shouldn't store client state

exercise.prompt: Map the operation to the correct RESTful HTTP verb and URL.
hints: Use GET for reading, POST for creating, and DELETE for deleting
PATCH is used for partial updates (like changing just a bio), while PUT often replaces the whole resource
exercise.test_code:
assert "GET" in api_design["Get a specific user"]
assert "POST" in api_design["Create a new post"]
assert "PATCH" in api_design.get("Update user bio", "PUT") # allow PUT or PATCH for flexibility but PATCH preferred here
assert "DELETE" in api_design["Delete a comment"]
print("PASS")
